articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds 'Right to be Forgotten' with Caveats, Bolstering Digital Privacy
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a landmark judgment recognizing the 'Right to be Forgotten' as an intrinsic part of the Right to Privacy under Article 21.
• The ruling mandates a framework for individuals to request removal of personal data from public platforms and search engines under certain conditions.
• It balances individual privacy rights with freedom of speech and public interest, particularly for historical records and public figures.
• The judgment calls for legislative action to provide clear guidelines and a robust redressal mechanism.
• This decision is expected to significantly impact data protection laws and the digital economy in India.
prelimsPoints: Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty)
Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared Right to Privacy as a fundamental right
Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act) 2023 provisions related to data erasure
Difference between 'Right to be Forgotten' and 'Right to Erasure'
mainsPoints: Discuss the implications of the ruling on individual autonomy and digital freedom.
Analyze the challenges in balancing privacy with freedom of expression and public access to information.
Examine the role of the judiciary in evolving fundamental rights in the digital age.
Suggest legislative measures needed to operationalize the 'Right to be Forgotten' effectively.
Impact on internet intermediaries and data fiduciaries.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a digital data footprint being erased or obscured, symbolizing the 'Right to be Forgotten'.
articles.trivia: The 'Right to be Forgotten' was first established in European Union law by the Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) in 2014, in the case of Google Spain SL and Google Inc. v Agencia Española de Protección de Datos (AEPD) and Mario Costeja González.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Persistent Inflation Concerns
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at its latest review meeting.
• The decision was influenced by persistent inflationary pressures, particularly from food prices, despite a stable growth outlook.
• RBI reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target while supporting economic growth.
• Analysts anticipate a cautious approach from the central bank, with potential rate cuts pushed further into the future.
• Liquidity management measures were also discussed to ensure financial stability.
prelimsPoints: Current repo rate and its significance
Composition and function of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC)
Tools of monetary policy (repo, reverse repo, CRR, SLR, OMO)
Headline vs. Core inflation
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by RBI in balancing inflation control and growth objectives.
Discuss the impact of sustained high food inflation on household consumption and economic stability.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current monetary policy stance in the prevailing economic conditions.
Suggest alternative fiscal or supply-side measures to complement monetary policy in managing inflation.
articles.imageDescription: RBI logo with graphs showing economic indicators like inflation and interest rates.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India consists of six members: three officials from the RBI and three external experts nominated by the Government of India.

articles.headline: India-EU Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Show Significant Progress
articles.explainer:
• Negotiations for the India-European Union Free Trade Agreement (FTA) have reportedly made substantial headway, with key areas nearing consensus.
• Breakthroughs are expected in contentious sectors like services, digital trade, and intellectual property rights.
• The FTA aims to boost bilateral trade and investment, creating new opportunities for businesses in both regions.
• Environmental and labour standards remain crucial discussion points, reflecting modern trade agreement norms.
• A potential deal could significantly diversify India's export basket and enhance its strategic partnership with the EU.
prelimsPoints: Key objectives of India's FTAs
Major trading partners of India and EU
Role of WTO in trade agreements
Difference between FTA, CEPA, PTA
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic significance of a comprehensive India-EU FTA for India.
Discuss the potential challenges and opportunities for Indian industries post-FTA implementation.
Examine how the FTA can diversify India's trade relations away from traditional partners.
Evaluate the role of environmental and labour clauses in modern trade agreements.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and the European Union superimposed over a global trade map.
articles.trivia: The European Union is India's third-largest trading partner, accounting for 10.8% of India's total trade in goods in 2022-23.

articles.headline: India Unveils Ambitious New Targets for National Green Hydrogen Mission
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government announced enhanced targets for the National Green Hydrogen Mission, aiming to accelerate production and adoption.
• New incentives and policy frameworks are being developed to attract significant private investment in the green hydrogen sector.
• The mission seeks to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security.
• Focus includes developing cost-effective electrolysis technologies and creating demand across industrial sectors.
• This move aligns with India's commitment to achieving net-zero emissions by 2070 and reducing fossil fuel imports.
prelimsPoints: Definition of Green Hydrogen, Blue Hydrogen, Grey Hydrogen
India's target for net-zero emissions
Key components and objectives of the National Green Hydrogen Mission
Role of electrolysers in green hydrogen production
mainsPoints: Analyze the economic and environmental benefits of India's accelerated green hydrogen targets.
Discuss the technological and infrastructural challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption.
Examine the potential of green hydrogen in decarbonizing hard-to-abate sectors like steel, cement, and fertilizers.
Evaluate the government's policy support and incentives required to make India a global leader in green hydrogen.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting green hydrogen production facilities with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
articles.trivia: Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity, a process called electrolysis, emitting no greenhouse gases.

articles.headline: Delimitation Commission Begins Work in Select North-Eastern States: Focus on Fair Representation
articles.explainer:
• A Delimitation Commission has commenced its work in certain North-Eastern states to redraw assembly and parliamentary constituencies.
• The exercise aims to ensure fair representation based on the latest census data while respecting geographical and administrative boundaries.
• The process involves extensive consultations with political parties, civil society groups, and local stakeholders.
• Special provisions regarding Scheduled Tribes (ST) and Scheduled Castes (SC) representation are a key consideration in these regions.
• The move is expected to address long-standing demands for equitable representation but also faces potential political sensitivities.
prelimsPoints: Constitutional basis for delimitation (Articles 82 and 170)
Powers and functions of the Delimitation Commission
Last delimitation exercise in India
Exceptions to delimitation (e.g., J&K, Assam, Arunachal Pradesh, Manipur, Nagaland)
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and challenges of conducting delimitation exercises in diverse regions like the North-East.
Analyze the potential impact of redrawing constituencies on the political landscape and regional dynamics.
Evaluate how delimitation addresses issues of under-representation or over-representation of certain communities.
Examine the role of the Delimitation Commission in ensuring free and fair elections.
articles.imageDescription: Map of North-Eastern states showing potential redrawing of electoral boundaries.
articles.trivia: The orders of the Delimitation Commission have the force of law and cannot be challenged in any court.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Advanced Test of Reusable Launch Vehicle Technology
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved a significant milestone with the successful advanced test of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology.
• The test focused on autonomous landing capabilities and precise navigation, crucial for future space missions.
• This technology aims to drastically reduce the cost of space launches, making space access more affordable and sustainable.
• Development of RLV is a key component of India's long-term strategy for space exploration and commercial space market.
• The success positions India among a select few nations developing such advanced space technologies.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in Science & Technology
prelimsPoints: ISRO's RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) program
Benefits of reusable launch vehicles (cost reduction, sustainability)
Other countries developing RLV technology (SpaceX, Blue Origin)
Upcoming ISRO missions (Gaganyaan, Chandrayaan-4)
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle technology for India's space program.
Analyze how RLV can democratize access to space and foster commercial space ventures.
Examine the technological challenges involved in developing and deploying RLV systems.
Evaluate India's position in the global space race with such advancements.
articles.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle landing back on Earth.
articles.trivia: ISRO's RLV-TD program aims to realize a Two Stage To Orbit (TSTO) fully reusable launch vehicle.

articles.headline: National Education Policy 2020: Focus on Integrating Vocational Training from Early Stages
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education highlighted accelerated efforts to integrate vocational training into mainstream schooling under NEP 2020.
• The policy aims to introduce vocational education from Class 6 onwards, providing practical skills and career readiness.
• Partnerships with industries and local businesses are being forged to offer apprenticeships and hands-on experience.
• The objective is to bridge the skill gap, enhance employability, and promote entrepreneurship among youth.
• This initiative seeks to transform India's education system to meet the demands of the 21st-century economy.
prelimsPoints: Key pillars of NEP 2020
Recommendations for vocational education in NEP
Role of National Skill Development Corporation (NSDC)
Schemes like PM Kaushal Vikas Yojana
mainsPoints: Analyze how NEP 2020's focus on vocational education can address India's demographic dividend and skill gap.
Discuss the challenges in implementing vocational training at early school levels across diverse regions.
Evaluate the potential impact on youth employability and entrepreneurship.
Suggest measures to ensure quality and industry relevance of vocational courses.
articles.imageDescription: Students engaged in vocational training activities like carpentry or electronics.
articles.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 replaced the 1986 National Policy on Education, which was modified in 1992.

articles.headline: Government Strengthens Cyber Security Framework for Critical Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) issued new directives to bolster cyber security for critical infrastructure sectors.
• The updated framework mandates stringent security audits, incident response protocols, and enhanced threat intelligence sharing.
• Key sectors include energy, finance, transportation, and telecommunications, crucial for national security and economic stability.
• The move comes in response to increasing sophisticated cyber threats and state-sponsored attacks.
• Capacity building and skill development programs for cyber security professionals are also part of the comprehensive strategy.
articles.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
keyTerms: National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC)
prelimsPoints: Definition of Critical Information Infrastructure (CII)
Role of NCIIPC and CERT-In
Key provisions of the IT Act, 2000 related to cyber security
Types of cyber attacks (ransomware, phishing, DDoS)
mainsPoints: Analyze the growing threat of cyber attacks on critical infrastructure and its implications for national security.
Discuss the effectiveness of India's current cyber security framework and the need for new directives.
Examine the challenges in securing critical infrastructure in an increasingly interconnected digital landscape.
Suggest measures to foster public-private partnership in strengthening cyber resilience.
articles.imageDescription: A network diagram showing critical infrastructure nodes protected by a digital shield.
articles.trivia: India ranks among the top countries most affected by cyberattacks, with critical infrastructure being a prime target.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Internal Security
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Challenges to Internal Security through Communication Networks

articles.headline: Agri-Tech Startups Witness Investment Surge, Transforming Indian Agriculture
articles.explainer:
• The Indian agri-tech sector is experiencing a significant boom, attracting substantial venture capital investments.
• Startups are leveraging technology like AI, IoT, and drones to enhance farm productivity, supply chain efficiency, and market access.
• Government initiatives and policies promoting agricultural innovation are fueling this growth.
• Innovations include precision farming, crop monitoring, digital marketplaces for farmers, and climate-resilient agriculture solutions.
• This transformation is crucial for doubling farmers' income and ensuring food security in India.
prelimsPoints: Government schemes supporting agri-tech (e.g., Agri-Stack, FPOs)
Role of NITI Aayog in promoting innovation
Examples of agri-tech innovations (e.g., soil sensors, drone spraying)
Concept of 'doubling farmers' income'
mainsPoints: Analyze how agri-tech startups are addressing the structural challenges in Indian agriculture.
Discuss the potential of technology to enhance farmers' income and improve agricultural sustainability.
Examine the role of government policy and private investment in fostering agri-tech innovation.
Suggest measures to ensure equitable access and adoption of agri-tech solutions among small and marginal farmers.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer using a tablet to monitor crop health, with drones flying over fields in the background.
articles.trivia: India's agricultural sector contributes approximately 18% to the country's GDP.

articles.headline: New Proposals Emerge for Enhanced Western Ghats Conservation, Balancing Ecology and Livelihoods
articles.explainer:
• Environmental experts and state governments are debating new proposals for strengthening conservation efforts in the Western Ghats.
• The discussions focus on delineating more effective Eco-Sensitive Zones (ESZs) and regulating developmental activities.
• Key challenge remains balancing ecological preservation with the livelihood needs of local communities and sustainable development.
• Proposals include stricter enforcement of environmental laws and promoting eco-tourism and sustainable agriculture.
• The Western Ghats is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a global biodiversity hotspot.
prelimsPoints: States covered by Western Ghats
Significance of Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot
Key recommendations of Gadgil and Kasturirangan reports
Concept of Eco-Sensitive Zones
mainsPoints: Analyze the ecological significance of the Western Ghats and the threats it faces.
Discuss the challenges in implementing conservation policies while addressing local community concerns.
Compare and contrast the recommendations of the Gadgil and Kasturirangan committees.
Suggest a balanced approach for sustainable development and conservation in the region.
articles.imageDescription: Lush green landscape of the Western Ghats with endemic flora and fauna.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas and are home to at least 325 globally threatened species.

articles.headline: Prison Reforms Committee Submits Comprehensive Report Recommending Holistic Changes
articles.explainer:
• A high-level committee on prison reforms submitted its report to the Ministry of Home Affairs, advocating for significant changes.
• Recommendations include addressing prison overcrowding, improving inmate welfare, and enhancing rehabilitation programs.
• The report emphasizes the need for modernizing prison infrastructure and leveraging technology for better administration.
• Focus on decriminalization of minor offenses and alternative dispute resolution to reduce pressure on the justice system.
• The reforms aim to align Indian prisons with international human rights standards and promote restorative justice.
prelimsPoints: Prison is a state subject (Seventh Schedule)
Key issues in Indian prisons (overcrowding, under-trials)
Concept of open prisons
Role of National Legal Services Authority (NALSA) in providing legal aid to prisoners
mainsPoints: Analyze the major challenges facing the Indian prison system and their socio-economic implications.
Discuss how comprehensive prison reforms can contribute to effective criminal justice administration and human rights.
Evaluate the role of technology and alternative dispute resolution in reducing prison population and improving inmate welfare.
Suggest measures for successful rehabilitation and reintegration of prisoners into society.
articles.imageDescription: An illustration showing a prison cell transforming into a rehabilitation center, symbolizing reform.
articles.trivia: India's prison occupancy rate often exceeds 115%, with under-trial prisoners constituting over two-thirds of the inmate population.

articles.headline: India Strengthens Partnership with African Union on Food Security and Climate Resilience
articles.explainer:
• India announced new initiatives to bolster cooperation with the African Union (AU) in areas of food security and climate resilience.
• The partnership focuses on sharing agricultural best practices, technology transfer, and capacity building for sustainable farming.
• Joint research and development projects are planned to develop climate-resilient crop varieties suitable for African conditions.
• India's commitment aligns with its vision of 'South-South Cooperation' and strengthening ties with the Global South.
• This collaboration aims to address common challenges posed by climate change and ensure food security for millions.
prelimsPoints: India's 'Act East' policy and its relevance to Africa
Key initiatives of India-Africa Forum Summit
Role of AU in continental integration
SDGs related to food security and climate action
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of India's growing engagement with the African Union.
Discuss how India's cooperation on food security and climate resilience benefits both regions.
Evaluate the potential of South-South Cooperation in addressing global challenges like climate change and poverty.
Examine the role of technology transfer and capacity building in sustainable agricultural development in Africa.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and African Union member states with images of agriculture and renewable energy.
articles.trivia: India has provided concessional Lines of Credit worth over US$12.3 billion to African countries for various development projects.

articles.headline: AI Advancements Revolutionize Disaster Prediction and Management in India
articles.explainer:
• Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) are increasingly being deployed to enhance India's disaster prediction and management capabilities.
• New AI models are showing promising results in forecasting extreme weather events, floods, and droughts with greater accuracy.
• Real-time data analysis and predictive analytics enable quicker response times and more effective resource allocation.
• Government agencies are collaborating with tech companies and research institutions to integrate AI into disaster warning systems.
• This technological leap aims to minimize loss of life and property, improving overall disaster resilience.
prelimsPoints: Role of NDMA and State Disaster Management Authorities (SDMAs)
Types of natural disasters in India
Applications of AI in various sectors
Importance of early warning systems
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential of AI and ML in transforming India's approach to disaster prediction and response.
Discuss the challenges and ethical considerations in deploying AI for disaster management.
Evaluate how AI can contribute to building more resilient communities against climate change impacts.
Suggest policy measures to promote the adoption of advanced technologies in disaster risk reduction.
articles.imageDescription: A satellite image showing weather patterns, overlaid with AI-generated predictions of a storm's path.
articles.trivia: India's National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) was established in 2005 under the Disaster Management Act.

articles.headline: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Textiles Boosts Domestic Manufacturing and Exports
articles.explainer:
• The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for the textile sector is showing promising results, leading to increased domestic manufacturing.
• The scheme aims to boost investment in Man-Made Fibre (MMF) apparel, MMF fabrics, and technical textiles.
• It offers incentives to eligible manufacturers, encouraging scaling up production and enhancing India's competitiveness globally.
• The initiative is creating new job opportunities and promoting advanced technology adoption in the textile industry.
• This aligns with the 'Make in India' vision and India's goal to become a global textile manufacturing hub.
prelimsPoints: Objectives of the PLI scheme across various sectors
Key features of the PLI scheme for textiles
Significance of the textile sector in India's economy
Difference between natural and man-made fibres
mainsPoints: Analyze the effectiveness of the PLI scheme in boosting domestic manufacturing and exports in the textile sector.
Discuss the challenges faced by the Indian textile industry and how PLI addresses them.
Evaluate the role of such incentive schemes in achieving the 'Make in India' and 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' objectives.
Suggest further policy interventions to enhance the competitiveness of India's textile industry globally.
articles.imageDescription: A textile factory floor with modern machinery and workers, symbolizing increased production.
articles.trivia: The Indian textile industry is one of the oldest and largest in the world, employing over 45 million people directly.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Significant Harappan-Era Settlement in Gujarat
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of a large Harappan-era settlement in the Kutch region of Gujarat.
• Initial findings suggest a well-planned urban center with evidence of sophisticated water management systems and craftsmanship.
• The site is expected to provide new insights into the socio-economic structure and cultural practices of the Indus Valley Civilization.
• Excavations are ongoing, with further research anticipated to reveal connections with other major Harappan sites.
• This discovery adds to the rich archaeological heritage of Gujarat and India, offering valuable historical data.
prelimsPoints: Key features of Harappan civilization (urban planning, drainage, seals)
Major Harappan sites in India (Dholavira, Lothal, Rakhigarhi)
Chronology of IVC (Early, Mature, Late Harappan)
Role of ASI in preservation of heritage
mainsPoints: Analyze how new archaeological discoveries contribute to our understanding of the Indus Valley Civilization.
Discuss the unique features of Harappan urban planning and water management systems.
Evaluate the significance of Gujarat as a region for Harappan archaeology.
Examine the challenges and ethical considerations in archaeological excavations and preservation.
articles.imageDescription: Archaeologists at an excavation site, carefully unearthing artifacts from a historical settlement.
articles.trivia: Dholavira, located in Gujarat, is one of the five largest Harappan sites and was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2021.

articles.headline: Debate on Uniform Civil Code Gains Momentum Ahead of Monsoon Session
articles.explainer:
• Discussions around the implementation of a Uniform Civil Code (UCC) have intensified, with political parties and legal experts weighing in.
• The government is reportedly considering introducing a bill or white paper on UCC during the upcoming monsoon session of Parliament.
• The UCC aims to replace diverse personal laws based on religion, custom, and tradition with a common set of laws governing marriage, divorce, inheritance, and adoption.
• Proponents argue for national unity and gender justice, while opponents raise concerns about religious freedom and cultural diversity.
• The Law Commission of India's recommendations and public consultations are expected to inform the legislative process.
prelimsPoints: Article 44 of the Indian Constitution
Goa Civil Code as an example of UCC
Shah Bano case and Sarla Mudgal case related to UCC
Difference between Fundamental Rights and DPSPs
mainsPoints: Analyze the constitutional basis and historical arguments for and against the Uniform Civil Code in India.
Discuss the potential benefits of UCC in promoting gender justice and national integration.
Examine the challenges and concerns raised regarding religious freedom and cultural diversity in the context of UCC.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary and Law Commission in shaping the debate around UCC.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel and legal books, with symbols representing different religions converging, symbolizing a common law.
articles.trivia: Goa is the only Indian state with a Uniform Civil Code, inherited from its Portuguese colonial past.

articles.headline: India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEC) Progresses with Infrastructure Planning
articles.explainer:
• Planning for the India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEC) is advancing, with focus on detailed infrastructure development.
• IMEC aims to connect India with Europe via the Middle East through a network of railways and shipping lanes, enhancing trade and connectivity.
• The project, announced at the G20 Summit, involves key partners like the US, Saudi Arabia, UAE, France, Germany, and Italy.
• It is envisioned to diversify global supply chains, reduce transit times, and foster regional economic development.
• Geopolitical implications and strategic competition with other connectivity initiatives are also under consideration.
prelimsPoints: Countries involved in IMEC
Objectives of IMEC
Comparison with China's BRI
Significance of G20 in global governance
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic significance of the IMEC for India and its partners.
Discuss how IMEC can reshape global trade routes and enhance supply chain resilience.
Examine the geopolitical implications of IMEC in the context of broader power competition.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in implementing a large-scale infrastructure project like IMEC.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized map showing the IMEC route connecting India, the Middle East, and Europe.
articles.trivia: The IMEC was announced during the G20 Summit in New Delhi in September 2023.

articles.headline: AI Integration in Ayushman Bharat: Enhancing Accessibility and Efficiency of Healthcare
articles.explainer:
• The National Health Authority (NHA) is exploring advanced AI and ML solutions to improve the delivery and efficiency of Ayushman Bharat.
• AI applications include predictive analytics for disease outbreaks, personalized treatment recommendations, and fraud detection.
• Telemedicine services under Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) are being augmented with AI-powered diagnostics.
• The goal is to make healthcare more accessible, affordable, and equitable, especially in remote and underserved areas.
• This digital transformation aims to strengthen India's public health infrastructure and patient outcomes.
keyTerms: Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-PMJAY)
prelimsPoints: Objectives of Ayushman Bharat (PMJAY and Health & Wellness Centres)
Key components of ABDM (Ayushman Bharat Health Account)
Benefits of AI in healthcare
Challenges in digital health adoption
mainsPoints: Analyze how AI integration can enhance the reach and effectiveness of Ayushman Bharat programs.
Discuss the ethical considerations and data privacy concerns associated with AI in healthcare.
Evaluate the potential of digital health initiatives to bridge the urban-rural healthcare divide in India.
Suggest policy frameworks to ensure equitable access and responsible deployment of AI in public health.
articles.imageDescription: A doctor interacting with a patient via a telemedicine screen, with AI interface elements.
articles.trivia: Ayushman Bharat is the world's largest government-funded healthcare program, covering over 50 crore beneficiaries.

articles.headline: India's Indigenous Defence Production Surges, Boosting 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' in Defence Sector
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence announced a significant increase in indigenous defence production, achieving new milestones in self-reliance.
• This surge is attributed to revised defence procurement policies, increased R&D investment, and private sector participation.
• New platforms, systems, and components are being designed and manufactured domestically, reducing import dependency.
• The 'positive indigenisation lists' have been instrumental in encouraging local production and fostering defence innovation.
• This push not only strengthens national security but also creates jobs and stimulates economic growth.
prelimsPoints: Objectives of 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' in defence
Role of Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO)
Examples of indigenously developed defence platforms (e.g., LCA Tejas, INS Vikrant)
Concept of 'positive indigenisation list'
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of indigenous defence production for India's national security and geopolitical standing.
Discuss the policy initiatives and reforms undertaken to boost self-reliance in the defence sector.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the domestic defence industry in achieving complete indigenization.
Suggest measures to further encourage private sector participation and foster innovation in defence manufacturing.
articles.imageDescription: Modern fighter jets and naval ships being manufactured in an Indian defence facility.
articles.trivia: India aims to achieve a defence manufacturing turnover of USD 25 billion by 2025, including exports of USD 5 billion.

highlights: Supreme Court's landmark ruling upholds 'Right to be Forgotten' as part of digital privacy, mandating a framework for data removal requests.
RBI maintains status quo on repo rate amidst persistent inflation concerns, signaling a cautious approach to monetary policy.
India-EU Free Trade Agreement negotiations show significant progress, with breakthroughs expected in services and digital trade.
Government unveils ambitious new targets for the National Green Hydrogen Mission, aiming to establish India as a global hub.
IMEC (India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor) infrastructure planning advances, promising to reshape global trade routes.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'Right to be Forgotten', consider the following statements:
1. It is explicitly mentioned as a fundamental right under Article 19 of the Indian Constitution.
2. The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 includes provisions related to data erasure requests.
3. The European Union was the first to legally establish this right through a landmark court ruling.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. The 'Right to be Forgotten' is considered an intrinsic part of the Right to Privacy under Article 21, as interpreted by the Supreme Court, not explicitly mentioned under Article 19. Statement 2 is correct. The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, includes provisions for the right to erasure and correction of personal data. Statement 3 is correct. The 'Right to be Forgotten' was first established in EU law by the CJEU in 2014.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the National Green Hydrogen Mission:
1. Its primary objective is to make India a global hub for the production and export of Green Hydrogen.
2. Green Hydrogen is produced using renewable energy sources to split water through electrolysis.
3. The mission aims to completely replace fossil fuels in India's energy mix by 2030.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. A key objective of the National Green Hydrogen Mission is to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export. Statement 2 is correct. Green hydrogen is indeed produced by electrolysis of water using renewable electricity. Statement 3 is incorrect. While the mission aims to reduce fossil fuel dependence and achieve net-zero by 2070, it does not aim for a complete replacement of fossil fuels by 2030, which is an unrealistic short-term target.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following bodies is responsible for the delimitation of parliamentary and assembly constituencies in India?
(a) Election Commission of India
(b) Parliament of India
(c) Delimitation Commission
(d) Ministry of Law and Justice
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Delimitation Commission is a high-powered body constituted by the Government of India under the Delimitation Commission Act. Its main task is to redraw the boundaries of parliamentary and assembly constituencies to ensure that all constituencies, as far as practicable, have the same population and provide fair representation.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to India's space program, what is the primary benefit of developing Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology?
(a) To achieve faster orbital insertion for satellites.
(b) To enable human spaceflight missions to Mars.
(c) To significantly reduce the cost of space launches.
(d) To deploy larger payloads into geostationary orbit.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) To significantly reduce the cost of space launches.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The primary benefit and objective of developing Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology is to drastically reduce the cost associated with space launches. By making launch vehicles reusable, the significant expense of building a new rocket for each mission can be avoided, leading to more affordable and frequent access to space.

prelimsMocks.question:
Article 44 of the Indian Constitution, which deals with the Uniform Civil Code, is a part of which of the following?
(a) Fundamental Rights
(b) Directive Principles of State Policy
(c) Fundamental Duties
(d) Eleventh Schedule
prelimsMocks.explanation: Article 44, which states that 'The State shall endeavour to secure for the citizens a Uniform Civil Code throughout the territory of India,' is a part of Part IV of the Indian Constitution, which deals with the Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSP). DPSPs are non-justiciable but fundamental in the governance of the country.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's recognition of the 'Right to be Forgotten' marks a significant step towards reinforcing digital privacy in India. Discuss the implications of this ruling for individual rights, freedom of expression, and the responsibilities of online platforms. (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Fundamental Rights, Judiciary, IT & Cyber Security
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the 'Right to be Forgotten' and its constitutional basis (Article 21, Puttaswamy judgment). Discuss positive implications: enhanced individual privacy, control over personal data, protection against reputational harm. Discuss challenges/caveats: balancing with freedom of speech (Article 19), public interest, historical accuracy, technical feasibility for platforms. Mention the need for legislative clarity (DPDP Act). Conclude on the evolving nature of rights in the digital age.

mainsMocks.question: India's National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to establish the country as a global leader in green hydrogen production. Critically analyze the potential benefits and challenges associated with achieving this ambition, particularly concerning technological readiness, infrastructure development, and economic viability. (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Environment, Energy, Infrastructure, Science & Technology
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the National Green Hydrogen Mission and its ambitious goals. Benefits: energy security, decarbonization of hard-to-abate sectors, climate action, economic growth, job creation. Challenges: high production cost (electrolysers), infrastructure for storage/transport, technology maturity, water availability for electrolysis, competition from other energy sources, policy support for demand creation. Conclude with the need for sustained policy push, R&D, and international collaboration.

mainsMocks.question: The debate around the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) in India often pits the ideals of national unity and gender justice against concerns for religious freedom and cultural diversity. Analyze these contrasting perspectives and suggest a pathway for a progressive and inclusive approach to personal law reform. (15 marks, 250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Directive Principles of State Policy, Fundamental Rights, Social Justice
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce UCC (Article 44) and its objectives. Arguments for: gender equality, national integration, secularism, simplifying legal system. Arguments against: threat to religious freedom (Article 25), cultural diversity, minority rights, impracticality in a diverse nation. Pathway: focus on piecemeal reforms, build consensus through dialogue, prioritize gender-just provisions, consider a phased implementation, learn from Goa Civil Code, involve community leaders. Conclude by emphasizing dialogue, inclusivity, and constitutional values.

quickBytes.text: The 'Right to be Forgotten' is typically applied to outdated, irrelevant, or excessive personal data.
The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) comprises 6 members, 3 from RBI and 3 external members.
India's net-zero emissions target is set for the year 2070.
The Delimitation Commission's orders cannot be challenged in any court of law.
ISRO's RLV-TD program is a step towards developing a Two Stage To Orbit (TSTO) reusable launch vehicle.
NEP 2020 aims to integrate vocational education from Class 6 onwards.
NCIIPC (National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre) is the nodal agency for CII protection.
The Western Ghats is one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.
Prison is a 'State Subject' under the Seventh Schedule of the Indian Constitution.
Goa is the only Indian state with a Uniform Civil Code.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unearths Significant Harappan-Era Settlement in Gujarat
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of a large Harappan-era settlement in the Kutch region of Gujarat.
• Initial findings suggest a well-planned urban center with evidence of sophisticated water management systems and craftsmanship.
• The site is expected to provide new insights into the socio-economic structure and cultural practices of the Indus Valley Civilization.
• Excavations are ongoing, with further research anticipated to reveal connections with other major Harappan sites.
• This discovery adds to the rich archaeological heritage of Gujarat and India, offering valuable historical data.
prelimsPoints: Key features of Harappan civilization (urban planning, drainage, seals)
Major Harappan sites in India (Dholavira, Lothal, Rakhigarhi)
Chronology of IVC (Early, Mature, Late Harappan)
Role of ASI in preservation of heritage
mainsPoints: Analyze how new archaeological discoveries contribute to our understanding of the Indus Valley Civilization.
Discuss the unique features of Harappan urban planning and water management systems.
Evaluate the significance of Gujarat as a region for Harappan archaeology.
Examine the challenges and ethical considerations in archaeological excavations and preservation.
GS1.imageDescription: Archaeologists at an excavation site, carefully unearthing artifacts from a historical settlement.
GS1.trivia: Dholavira, located in Gujarat, is one of the five largest Harappan sites and was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2021.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds 'Right to be Forgotten' with Caveats, Bolstering Digital Privacy
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a landmark judgment recognizing the 'Right to be Forgotten' as an intrinsic part of the Right to Privacy under Article 21.
• The ruling mandates a framework for individuals to request removal of personal data from public platforms and search engines under certain conditions.
• It balances individual privacy rights with freedom of speech and public interest, particularly for historical records and public figures.
• The judgment calls for legislative action to provide clear guidelines and a robust redressal mechanism.
• This decision is expected to significantly impact data protection laws and the digital economy in India.
prelimsPoints: Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty)
Puttaswamy judgment (2017) declared Right to Privacy as a fundamental right
Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act) 2023 provisions related to data erasure
Difference between 'Right to be Forgotten' and 'Right to Erasure'
mainsPoints: Discuss the implications of the ruling on individual autonomy and digital freedom.
Analyze the challenges in balancing privacy with freedom of expression and public access to information.
Examine the role of the judiciary in evolving fundamental rights in the digital age.
Suggest legislative measures needed to operationalize the 'Right to be Forgotten' effectively.
Impact on internet intermediaries and data fiduciaries.
GS2.imageDescription: Illustration of a digital data footprint being erased or obscured, symbolizing the 'Right to be Forgotten'.
GS2.trivia: The 'Right to be Forgotten' was first established in European Union law by the Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) in 2014, in the case of Google Spain SL and Google Inc. v Agencia Española de Protección de Datos (AEPD) and Mario Costeja González.

GS2.headline: India-EU Free Trade Agreement Negotiations Show Significant Progress
GS2.explainer:
• Negotiations for the India-European Union Free Trade Agreement (FTA) have reportedly made substantial headway, with key areas nearing consensus.
• Breakthroughs are expected in contentious sectors like services, digital trade, and intellectual property rights.
• The FTA aims to boost bilateral trade and investment, creating new opportunities for businesses in both regions.
• Environmental and labour standards remain crucial discussion points, reflecting modern trade agreement norms.
• A potential deal could significantly diversify India's export basket and enhance its strategic partnership with the EU.
prelimsPoints: Key objectives of India's FTAs
Major trading partners of India and EU
Role of WTO in trade agreements
Difference between FTA, CEPA, PTA
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic significance of a comprehensive India-EU FTA for India.
Discuss the potential challenges and opportunities for Indian industries post-FTA implementation.
Examine how the FTA can diversify India's trade relations away from traditional partners.
Evaluate the role of environmental and labour clauses in modern trade agreements.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and the European Union superimposed over a global trade map.
GS2.trivia: The European Union is India's third-largest trading partner, accounting for 10.8% of India's total trade in goods in 2022-23.

GS2.headline: Delimitation Commission Begins Work in Select North-Eastern States: Focus on Fair Representation
GS2.explainer:
• A Delimitation Commission has commenced its work in certain North-Eastern states to redraw assembly and parliamentary constituencies.
• The exercise aims to ensure fair representation based on the latest census data while respecting geographical and administrative boundaries.
• The process involves extensive consultations with political parties, civil society groups, and local stakeholders.
• Special provisions regarding Scheduled Tribes (ST) and Scheduled Castes (SC) representation are a key consideration in these regions.
• The move is expected to address long-standing demands for equitable representation but also faces potential political sensitivities.
prelimsPoints: Constitutional basis for delimitation (Articles 82 and 170)
Powers and functions of the Delimitation Commission
Last delimitation exercise in India
Exceptions to delimitation (e.g., J&K, Assam, Arunachal Pradesh, Manipur, Nagaland)
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and challenges of conducting delimitation exercises in diverse regions like the North-East.
Analyze the potential impact of redrawing constituencies on the political landscape and regional dynamics.
Evaluate how delimitation addresses issues of under-representation or over-representation of certain communities.
Examine the role of the Delimitation Commission in ensuring free and fair elections.
GS2.imageDescription: Map of North-Eastern states showing potential redrawing of electoral boundaries.
GS2.trivia: The orders of the Delimitation Commission have the force of law and cannot be challenged in any court.

GS2.headline: National Education Policy 2020: Focus on Integrating Vocational Training from Early Stages
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education highlighted accelerated efforts to integrate vocational training into mainstream schooling under NEP 2020.
• The policy aims to introduce vocational education from Class 6 onwards, providing practical skills and career readiness.
• Partnerships with industries and local businesses are being forged to offer apprenticeships and hands-on experience.
• The objective is to bridge the skill gap, enhance employability, and promote entrepreneurship among youth.
• This initiative seeks to transform India's education system to meet the demands of the 21st-century economy.
prelimsPoints: Key pillars of NEP 2020
Recommendations for vocational education in NEP
Role of National Skill Development Corporation (NSDC)
Schemes like PM Kaushal Vikas Yojana
mainsPoints: Analyze how NEP 2020's focus on vocational education can address India's demographic dividend and skill gap.
Discuss the challenges in implementing vocational training at early school levels across diverse regions.
Evaluate the potential impact on youth employability and entrepreneurship.
Suggest measures to ensure quality and industry relevance of vocational courses.
GS2.imageDescription: Students engaged in vocational training activities like carpentry or electronics.
GS2.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 replaced the 1986 National Policy on Education, which was modified in 1992.

GS2.headline: Prison Reforms Committee Submits Comprehensive Report Recommending Holistic Changes
GS2.explainer:
• A high-level committee on prison reforms submitted its report to the Ministry of Home Affairs, advocating for significant changes.
• Recommendations include addressing prison overcrowding, improving inmate welfare, and enhancing rehabilitation programs.
• The report emphasizes the need for modernizing prison infrastructure and leveraging technology for better administration.
• Focus on decriminalization of minor offenses and alternative dispute resolution to reduce pressure on the justice system.
• The reforms aim to align Indian prisons with international human rights standards and promote restorative justice.
prelimsPoints: Prison is a state subject (Seventh Schedule)
Key issues in Indian prisons (overcrowding, under-trials)
Concept of open prisons
Role of National Legal Services Authority (NALSA) in providing legal aid to prisoners
mainsPoints: Analyze the major challenges facing the Indian prison system and their socio-economic implications.
Discuss how comprehensive prison reforms can contribute to effective criminal justice administration and human rights.
Evaluate the role of technology and alternative dispute resolution in reducing prison population and improving inmate welfare.
Suggest measures for successful rehabilitation and reintegration of prisoners into society.
GS2.imageDescription: An illustration showing a prison cell transforming into a rehabilitation center, symbolizing reform.
GS2.trivia: India's prison occupancy rate often exceeds 115%, with under-trial prisoners constituting over two-thirds of the inmate population.

GS2.headline: India Strengthens Partnership with African Union on Food Security and Climate Resilience
GS2.explainer:
• India announced new initiatives to bolster cooperation with the African Union (AU) in areas of food security and climate resilience.
• The partnership focuses on sharing agricultural best practices, technology transfer, and capacity building for sustainable farming.
• Joint research and development projects are planned to develop climate-resilient crop varieties suitable for African conditions.
• India's commitment aligns with its vision of 'South-South Cooperation' and strengthening ties with the Global South.
• This collaboration aims to address common challenges posed by climate change and ensure food security for millions.
prelimsPoints: India's 'Act East' policy and its relevance to Africa
Key initiatives of India-Africa Forum Summit
Role of AU in continental integration
SDGs related to food security and climate action
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of India's growing engagement with the African Union.
Discuss how India's cooperation on food security and climate resilience benefits both regions.
Evaluate the potential of South-South Cooperation in addressing global challenges like climate change and poverty.
Examine the role of technology transfer and capacity building in sustainable agricultural development in Africa.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and African Union member states with images of agriculture and renewable energy.
GS2.trivia: India has provided concessional Lines of Credit worth over US$12.3 billion to African countries for various development projects.

GS2.headline: Debate on Uniform Civil Code Gains Momentum Ahead of Monsoon Session
GS2.explainer:
• Discussions around the implementation of a Uniform Civil Code (UCC) have intensified, with political parties and legal experts weighing in.
• The government is reportedly considering introducing a bill or white paper on UCC during the upcoming monsoon session of Parliament.
• The UCC aims to replace diverse personal laws based on religion, custom, and tradition with a common set of laws governing marriage, divorce, inheritance, and adoption.
• Proponents argue for national unity and gender justice, while opponents raise concerns about religious freedom and cultural diversity.
• The Law Commission of India's recommendations and public consultations are expected to inform the legislative process.
prelimsPoints: Article 44 of the Indian Constitution
Goa Civil Code as an example of UCC
Shah Bano case and Sarla Mudgal case related to UCC
Difference between Fundamental Rights and DPSPs
mainsPoints: Analyze the constitutional basis and historical arguments for and against the Uniform Civil Code in India.
Discuss the potential benefits of UCC in promoting gender justice and national integration.
Examine the challenges and concerns raised regarding religious freedom and cultural diversity in the context of UCC.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary and Law Commission in shaping the debate around UCC.
GS2.imageDescription: A gavel and legal books, with symbols representing different religions converging, symbolizing a common law.
GS2.trivia: Goa is the only Indian state with a Uniform Civil Code, inherited from its Portuguese colonial past.

GS2.headline: India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEC) Progresses with Infrastructure Planning
GS2.explainer:
• Planning for the India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor (IMEC) is advancing, with focus on detailed infrastructure development.
• IMEC aims to connect India with Europe via the Middle East through a network of railways and shipping lanes, enhancing trade and connectivity.
• The project, announced at the G20 Summit, involves key partners like the US, Saudi Arabia, UAE, France, Germany, and Italy.
• It is envisioned to diversify global supply chains, reduce transit times, and foster regional economic development.
• Geopolitical implications and strategic competition with other connectivity initiatives are also under consideration.
prelimsPoints: Countries involved in IMEC
Objectives of IMEC
Comparison with China's BRI
Significance of G20 in global governance
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic and economic significance of the IMEC for India and its partners.
Discuss how IMEC can reshape global trade routes and enhance supply chain resilience.
Examine the geopolitical implications of IMEC in the context of broader power competition.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in implementing a large-scale infrastructure project like IMEC.
GS2.imageDescription: A stylized map showing the IMEC route connecting India, the Middle East, and Europe.
GS2.trivia: The IMEC was announced during the G20 Summit in New Delhi in September 2023.

GS2.headline: AI Integration in Ayushman Bharat: Enhancing Accessibility and Efficiency of Healthcare
GS2.explainer:
• The National Health Authority (NHA) is exploring advanced AI and ML solutions to improve the delivery and efficiency of Ayushman Bharat.
• AI applications include predictive analytics for disease outbreaks, personalized treatment recommendations, and fraud detection.
• Telemedicine services under Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) are being augmented with AI-powered diagnostics.
• The goal is to make healthcare more accessible, affordable, and equitable, especially in remote and underserved areas.
• This digital transformation aims to strengthen India's public health infrastructure and patient outcomes.
keyTerms: Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-PMJAY)
prelimsPoints: Objectives of Ayushman Bharat (PMJAY and Health & Wellness Centres)
Key components of ABDM (Ayushman Bharat Health Account)
Benefits of AI in healthcare
Challenges in digital health adoption
mainsPoints: Analyze how AI integration can enhance the reach and effectiveness of Ayushman Bharat programs.
Discuss the ethical considerations and data privacy concerns associated with AI in healthcare.
Evaluate the potential of digital health initiatives to bridge the urban-rural healthcare divide in India.
Suggest policy frameworks to ensure equitable access and responsible deployment of AI in public health.
GS2.imageDescription: A doctor interacting with a patient via a telemedicine screen, with AI interface elements.
GS2.trivia: Ayushman Bharat is the world's largest government-funded healthcare program, covering over 50 crore beneficiaries.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Persistent Inflation Concerns
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at its latest review meeting.
• The decision was influenced by persistent inflationary pressures, particularly from food prices, despite a stable growth outlook.
• RBI reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target while supporting economic growth.
• Analysts anticipate a cautious approach from the central bank, with potential rate cuts pushed further into the future.
• Liquidity management measures were also discussed to ensure financial stability.
prelimsPoints: Current repo rate and its significance
Composition and function of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC)
Tools of monetary policy (repo, reverse repo, CRR, SLR, OMO)
Headline vs. Core inflation
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by RBI in balancing inflation control and growth objectives.
Discuss the impact of sustained high food inflation on household consumption and economic stability.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current monetary policy stance in the prevailing economic conditions.
Suggest alternative fiscal or supply-side measures to complement monetary policy in managing inflation.
GS3.imageDescription: RBI logo with graphs showing economic indicators like inflation and interest rates.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India consists of six members: three officials from the RBI and three external experts nominated by the Government of India.

GS3.headline: India Unveils Ambitious New Targets for National Green Hydrogen Mission
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government announced enhanced targets for the National Green Hydrogen Mission, aiming to accelerate production and adoption.
• New incentives and policy frameworks are being developed to attract significant private investment in the green hydrogen sector.
• The mission seeks to establish India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security.
• Focus includes developing cost-effective electrolysis technologies and creating demand across industrial sectors.
• This move aligns with India's commitment to achieving net-zero emissions by 2070 and reducing fossil fuel imports.
prelimsPoints: Definition of Green Hydrogen, Blue Hydrogen, Grey Hydrogen
India's target for net-zero emissions
Key components and objectives of the National Green Hydrogen Mission
Role of electrolysers in green hydrogen production
mainsPoints: Analyze the economic and environmental benefits of India's accelerated green hydrogen targets.
Discuss the technological and infrastructural challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production and adoption.
Examine the potential of green hydrogen in decarbonizing hard-to-abate sectors like steel, cement, and fertilizers.
Evaluate the government's policy support and incentives required to make India a global leader in green hydrogen.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic depicting green hydrogen production facilities with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
GS3.trivia: Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity, a process called electrolysis, emitting no greenhouse gases.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Advanced Test of Reusable Launch Vehicle Technology
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved a significant milestone with the successful advanced test of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology.
• The test focused on autonomous landing capabilities and precise navigation, crucial for future space missions.
• This technology aims to drastically reduce the cost of space launches, making space access more affordable and sustainable.
• Development of RLV is a key component of India's long-term strategy for space exploration and commercial space market.
• The success positions India among a select few nations developing such advanced space technologies.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in Science & Technology
prelimsPoints: ISRO's RLV-TD (Technology Demonstrator) program
Benefits of reusable launch vehicles (cost reduction, sustainability)
Other countries developing RLV technology (SpaceX, Blue Origin)
Upcoming ISRO missions (Gaganyaan, Chandrayaan-4)
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle technology for India's space program.
Analyze how RLV can democratize access to space and foster commercial space ventures.
Examine the technological challenges involved in developing and deploying RLV systems.
Evaluate India's position in the global space race with such advancements.
GS3.imageDescription: Artistic rendering of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle landing back on Earth.
GS3.trivia: ISRO's RLV-TD program aims to realize a Two Stage To Orbit (TSTO) fully reusable launch vehicle.

GS3.headline: Government Strengthens Cyber Security Framework for Critical Infrastructure
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) issued new directives to bolster cyber security for critical infrastructure sectors.
• The updated framework mandates stringent security audits, incident response protocols, and enhanced threat intelligence sharing.
• Key sectors include energy, finance, transportation, and telecommunications, crucial for national security and economic stability.
• The move comes in response to increasing sophisticated cyber threats and state-sponsored attacks.
• Capacity building and skill development programs for cyber security professionals are also part of the comprehensive strategy.
GS3.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
keyTerms: National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC)
prelimsPoints: Definition of Critical Information Infrastructure (CII)
Role of NCIIPC and CERT-In
Key provisions of the IT Act, 2000 related to cyber security
Types of cyber attacks (ransomware, phishing, DDoS)
mainsPoints: Analyze the growing threat of cyber attacks on critical infrastructure and its implications for national security.
Discuss the effectiveness of India's current cyber security framework and the need for new directives.
Examine the challenges in securing critical infrastructure in an increasingly interconnected digital landscape.
Suggest measures to foster public-private partnership in strengthening cyber resilience.
GS3.imageDescription: A network diagram showing critical infrastructure nodes protected by a digital shield.
GS3.trivia: India ranks among the top countries most affected by cyberattacks, with critical infrastructure being a prime target.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Internal Security
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Challenges to Internal Security through Communication Networks

GS3.headline: Agri-Tech Startups Witness Investment Surge, Transforming Indian Agriculture
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian agri-tech sector is experiencing a significant boom, attracting substantial venture capital investments.
• Startups are leveraging technology like AI, IoT, and drones to enhance farm productivity, supply chain efficiency, and market access.
• Government initiatives and policies promoting agricultural innovation are fueling this growth.
• Innovations include precision farming, crop monitoring, digital marketplaces for farmers, and climate-resilient agriculture solutions.
• This transformation is crucial for doubling farmers' income and ensuring food security in India.
prelimsPoints: Government schemes supporting agri-tech (e.g., Agri-Stack, FPOs)
Role of NITI Aayog in promoting innovation
Examples of agri-tech innovations (e.g., soil sensors, drone spraying)
Concept of 'doubling farmers' income'
mainsPoints: Analyze how agri-tech startups are addressing the structural challenges in Indian agriculture.
Discuss the potential of technology to enhance farmers' income and improve agricultural sustainability.
Examine the role of government policy and private investment in fostering agri-tech innovation.
Suggest measures to ensure equitable access and adoption of agri-tech solutions among small and marginal farmers.
GS3.imageDescription: A farmer using a tablet to monitor crop health, with drones flying over fields in the background.
GS3.trivia: India's agricultural sector contributes approximately 18% to the country's GDP.

GS3.headline: New Proposals Emerge for Enhanced Western Ghats Conservation, Balancing Ecology and Livelihoods
GS3.explainer:
• Environmental experts and state governments are debating new proposals for strengthening conservation efforts in the Western Ghats.
• The discussions focus on delineating more effective Eco-Sensitive Zones (ESZs) and regulating developmental activities.
• Key challenge remains balancing ecological preservation with the livelihood needs of local communities and sustainable development.
• Proposals include stricter enforcement of environmental laws and promoting eco-tourism and sustainable agriculture.
• The Western Ghats is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a global biodiversity hotspot.
prelimsPoints: States covered by Western Ghats
Significance of Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot
Key recommendations of Gadgil and Kasturirangan reports
Concept of Eco-Sensitive Zones
mainsPoints: Analyze the ecological significance of the Western Ghats and the threats it faces.
Discuss the challenges in implementing conservation policies while addressing local community concerns.
Compare and contrast the recommendations of the Gadgil and Kasturirangan committees.
Suggest a balanced approach for sustainable development and conservation in the region.
GS3.imageDescription: Lush green landscape of the Western Ghats with endemic flora and fauna.
GS3.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayas and are home to at least 325 globally threatened species.

GS3.headline: AI Advancements Revolutionize Disaster Prediction and Management in India
GS3.explainer:
• Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) are increasingly being deployed to enhance India's disaster prediction and management capabilities.
• New AI models are showing promising results in forecasting extreme weather events, floods, and droughts with greater accuracy.
• Real-time data analysis and predictive analytics enable quicker response times and more effective resource allocation.
• Government agencies are collaborating with tech companies and research institutions to integrate AI into disaster warning systems.
• This technological leap aims to minimize loss of life and property, improving overall disaster resilience.
prelimsPoints: Role of NDMA and State Disaster Management Authorities (SDMAs)
Types of natural disasters in India
Applications of AI in various sectors
Importance of early warning systems
mainsPoints: Analyze the potential of AI and ML in transforming India's approach to disaster prediction and response.
Discuss the challenges and ethical considerations in deploying AI for disaster management.
Evaluate how AI can contribute to building more resilient communities against climate change impacts.
Suggest policy measures to promote the adoption of advanced technologies in disaster risk reduction.
GS3.imageDescription: A satellite image showing weather patterns, overlaid with AI-generated predictions of a storm's path.
GS3.trivia: India's National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) was established in 2005 under the Disaster Management Act.

GS3.headline: Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Textiles Boosts Domestic Manufacturing and Exports
GS3.explainer:
• The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme for the textile sector is showing promising results, leading to increased domestic manufacturing.
• The scheme aims to boost investment in Man-Made Fibre (MMF) apparel, MMF fabrics, and technical textiles.
• It offers incentives to eligible manufacturers, encouraging scaling up production and enhancing India's competitiveness globally.
• The initiative is creating new job opportunities and promoting advanced technology adoption in the textile industry.
• This aligns with the 'Make in India' vision and India's goal to become a global textile manufacturing hub.
prelimsPoints: Objectives of the PLI scheme across various sectors
Key features of the PLI scheme for textiles
Significance of the textile sector in India's economy
Difference between natural and man-made fibres
mainsPoints: Analyze the effectiveness of the PLI scheme in boosting domestic manufacturing and exports in the textile sector.
Discuss the challenges faced by the Indian textile industry and how PLI addresses them.
Evaluate the role of such incentive schemes in achieving the 'Make in India' and 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' objectives.
Suggest further policy interventions to enhance the competitiveness of India's textile industry globally.
GS3.imageDescription: A textile factory floor with modern machinery and workers, symbolizing increased production.
GS3.trivia: The Indian textile industry is one of the oldest and largest in the world, employing over 45 million people directly.

GS3.headline: India's Indigenous Defence Production Surges, Boosting 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' in Defence Sector
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Defence announced a significant increase in indigenous defence production, achieving new milestones in self-reliance.
• This surge is attributed to revised defence procurement policies, increased R&D investment, and private sector participation.
• New platforms, systems, and components are being designed and manufactured domestically, reducing import dependency.
• The 'positive indigenisation lists' have been instrumental in encouraging local production and fostering defence innovation.
• This push not only strengthens national security but also creates jobs and stimulates economic growth.
prelimsPoints: Objectives of 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' in defence
Role of Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO)
Examples of indigenously developed defence platforms (e.g., LCA Tejas, INS Vikrant)
Concept of 'positive indigenisation list'
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of indigenous defence production for India's national security and geopolitical standing.
Discuss the policy initiatives and reforms undertaken to boost self-reliance in the defence sector.
Evaluate the challenges faced by the domestic defence industry in achieving complete indigenization.
Suggest measures to further encourage private sector participation and foster innovation in defence manufacturing.
GS3.imageDescription: Modern fighter jets and naval ships being manufactured in an Indian defence facility.
GS3.trivia: India aims to achieve a defence manufacturing turnover of USD 25 billion by 2025, including exports of USD 5 billion.

sources: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)
